revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Abuyama Kofun]][[fr:Kofun d'Abuyama]][[ja:阿武山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Ibaraki, Osaka, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Abuyama Kofun | native_name =阿武山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Abuyama Kofun burial chamber site | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Abuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.863333|N|135.569389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takatsuki, Osaka|Takatsuki]] and {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki, Osaka|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|51|48.04|N|135|34|9.82|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} [[File:阿武山古墳出土品.JPG|thumb|Artifacts uncovered from the tomb in Imashirotsuka Ancient History Museum]] [[File:Abuyaba-Kofun 01.jpg|thumb|Abuyama Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Abuyama Kofun'''|阿武山古墳||qq=}} is an {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} [[kofun|Kofun]], located on Mount Abu, on the border of the Nasahara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takatsuki, Osaka|Takatsuki]] and the Ai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. In 1934, the interred remains of an ancient nobleman were excavated from underground, and it has been hypothesized that the tomb was dedicated to the 7th century statesman {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, who is known as the founder of the powerful [[Fujiwara clan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.<ref>{{Cite web |title=国指定文化財等データベース |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1837 |access-date=2022-05-26 |website=Kunishitei Bunka National Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=史跡阿武山古墳／高槻市ホームページ |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/kofun/1327658589251.html |access-date=2021-07-21 |website=Takatsuki City, Osaka|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== In 1934, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] of an ancient Kofun was accidentally discovered during construction of the [[:en:Kyoto Imperial University|Kyoto Imperial University]]'s seismic observatory on the 281.1 meter Mount Abu. There was no indication at ground level that this was a ''kofun'', but further investigate determined that it had originally been a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|||qq=}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 82 meters, surrounded by a dry moat and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The burial chamber contained a coffin stand, on top of which was a sarcophagus made by hardening cloth with lacquer in multiple layers. The outside was painted with black lacquer and the inside with red lacquer. This was a unique discovery, made even more so when it was discovered that inside was the almost complete mummified remains of a man in his 60s, complete with hair and clothing. There were no mirrors, swords, jewels or other {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, but the body appeared to have been wrapped in brocade, with many gold threads were scattered from the chest to the head, which was resting on a pillow made of woven glass beads. On April 29, 1934, the discovery was made public by the {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Osaka Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}, and quickly attracted crowds of spectators due to speculation that it was the grave of {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} nobleman {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> However, there was a problem with the excavation. Kyoto University's Faculty of Science's Earthquake Observatory took the lead in excavating the site, but lacked knowledge of archeology and treated the ruins roughly. In response to this, the Kyoto University Archeology Laboratory, which was initially consulted but was not allowed to enter the site, and the Osaka Prefectural Government, which has jurisdiction over the ruins and was concerned about the rapid deterioration of the site filed complaints with the Ministry of Education, Ministry of Home Affairs and {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}. The central government ruled that as there was no question that the person buried at the site was of very high Rank, and was even possibly connected to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|imperial family]], further investigation would be considered as [[:en:lese majesty|lese majesty]] and ordered the site to be completely backfilled. Prior to this {{ill|Radiography|en|Radiography|qid=Q245341|lt=radiographic images|1=Radiography}} and samples were taken by the Kyoto University's Faculty of Science's Earthquake Observatory, but unwilling to share this information with the Kyoto University Archeology Laboratory, the data was filed away in the basement of the observatory without further analysis.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In 1982, when the seismic observatory was demolished, these old files were discovered. A 1987 analysis revealed that the deceased had a strong bone structure and an athletic body, with the so-called {{ill|Golfer's elbow|en|Golfer's elbow|qid=Q2457358|lt=pitcher's elbow|1=Golfer's elbow}}. The cause of death was complications from injuries to the [[:en:vertebral column|vertebral column]] and {{ill|lumbar vertebrae|en|lumbar vertebrae|qid=Q405478|lt=lumbar vertebrae|1=Lumbar vertebrae}} sustained from a fall, as if from horseback or a high ground. The injury is thought to have left the lower body paralyzed and could have caused secondary complications such as {{ill|Pneumonia|en|Pneumonia|qid=Q12192|lt=Pneumonia|1=Pneumonia}} or {{ill|urinary tract infection|en|urinary tract infection|qid=Q221668|lt=urinary tract infection|1=Urinary tract infection}}.<ref name=":0" /> The cause of death matches that of Kamatari's, who is recorded to have died from a fall from horseback.<ref name=":1" /> The distribution of gold threads was determined to have been from the embroidery thread for a ''taishokkan'' woven {{ill|Kanmuri (headwear)|en|Kanmuri (headwear)|qid=Q107039569|lt=''kanmuri''|1=Kanmuri (headwear)}}, which could have been used only by a noble of the highest Rank. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web |date=2013-12-14 |title=藤原鎌足の墓説さらに強まる 阿武山古墳に最高級の冠帽 |trans-title=The theory of the tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari further strengthens Highest grade crown cap in Abuyama Kofun |url=http://digital.asahi.com/articles/OSK201312120174.html |website={{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}|qq=}}</ref> Together with the lacquered coffins and the glass-ball pillows, it was concluded that it is highly likely that the tomb was that of the 7th century statesman and aristocrat {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=中臣鎌足（藤原鎌足） |trans-title=Nakatomi no Kamatari (Fujiwara no Kamatari) |url=http://archives.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/user_data/upload/File/ags/1-2-2-010.pdf |access-date=2022-05-26 |website=Yamaguchi Prefectural Archives|qq=}}</ref> In 1989, the area around the Kofun was developed as a historic park.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In October 2014, the Ibaraki City Education Committee announced that ancient ''sen'' bricks discovered at the [[Higashinara site]] in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki, Osaka|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} match the bricks found in Abuyama Kofun. The site is believed to have been the location of Mishima Betsugyō, a villa where Kamatari stayed before the {{ill|Isshi incident|en|Isshi incident|qid=Q1062771|lt=Isshi incident|1=Isshi incident}} which triggered the [[Taika Reform]] of 645 CE.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-10-10 |title=鎌足の隠居伝説を補強か 大阪の遺跡でゆかりの「れんが」 |trans-title=Reinforcing the legend of Kamatari's retreat? "Bricks" associated with the Osaka ruins |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASDG10030_Q4A011C1CR8000/ |access-date=2022-05-26 |website=[[The Nikkei]] |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Abuyama Kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/site/history/4566.html Takatsuki City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}]]]]]]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Abuyama Kofun]]
